main_functions.description: Allows a user to create an account using email and password with strict validation rules and handling of duplicate unverified emails.

sub_functions.description: Validates that the email is unique and properly formatted.

test_categories.category_name: Unique Email Check for Verified Accounts
test_categories.test_intent: Verify that registration fails with a generic error message when email already exists and is verified, without revealing whether the email is already registered.

test_categories.category_name: Unique Email Check for Unverified Accounts
test_categories.test_intent: Verify that registration proceeds normally from the frontend perspective when email exists but is unverified, showing a success message and not revealing that the email already exists.
Verify that registration rejects invalid email formats (e.g., missing @, invalid domain) with a generic error message.

sub_functions.description: Validates password against all complexity rules including length, character types, and restrictions.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that passwords shorter than 8 characters or longer than 12 characters are rejected with a generic error message.
Verify that passwords missing at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one numeric digit, or one special character are rejected with a generic error message.
Verify that passwords with consecutive spaces or containing the user's email prefix are rejected with a generic error message.

sub_functions.description: When a user registers with an email that already exists but is unverified, the system updates the record and resets verification.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that re-registering with an existing unverified email updates the password hash, generates a new verification token, and resets the 24-hour expiration clock.
Verify that the frontend shows a normal registration success message and does not reveal that the email already exists or that the record was updated.
Verify that simultaneous registration attempts with the same unverified email are handled consistently, resulting in a single updated record and no data corruption.

main_functions.description: Handles the verification of user email via a secure link with token validation, expiration checks, and error handling.

sub_functions.description: Generates a cryptographically secure random token, hashes it with SHA-256, and stores the hash in the database along with the issuance timestamp.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that the token is generated using a cryptographically secure random source and has sufficient entropy to prevent prediction.
Verify that the token is hashed with SHA-256 before being stored in the database, and that the raw token is never stored.
Verify that the token issuance timestamp is correctly set to the current server time and stored in the database for expiration calculation.

sub_functions.description: Processes the verification link by extracting the token, validating it against the stored hash, checking expiration, and marking the user as verified.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that a valid, non-expired token successfully verifies the email and marks the user as verified in the database.
Verify that an expired token (older than 24 hours from issuance) results in the message 'The verification link is invalid or has expired. Please request a new one.' and redirects to the resend page.

Verify that an invalid or tampered token (e.g., modified, random string) results in the message 'The verification link is invalid or has expired. Please request a new one.' and redirects to the resend page.
Verify that a token cannot be used more than once; after successful verification, reusing the same token fails with the invalid/expired message.
Verify that the system correctly hashes the incoming token with SHA-256 and compares it to the stored hash, and that a token with a different hash is rejected.
Verify that simultaneous verification requests with the same valid token are handled consistently, resulting in a single successful verification and subsequent requests failing.

sub_functions.description: Handles system errors during verification and enforces HTTPS and information hiding.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that on a system error during verification (e.g., database failure), the generic message 'An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.' is shown and no internal error details are exposed.
Verify that all verification traffic is forced over HTTPS (TLS 1.3) and that HTTP requests to verification endpoints are redirected or rejected.

sub_functions.description: Provides a mechanism for users to request a new verification email after an expired or invalid link.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that the 'Resend Verification Email' button is displayed on the landing page after an expired or invalid link is detected.
Verify that clicking the resend button generates a new token, updates the database with the new hash and issuance timestamp, and sends a new email with a fresh 24-hour expiration.
Verify that if the email delivery service fails during resend, the system logs the error and returns a generic error message to the user without exposing delivery details.

sub_functions.description: Sends the initial verification email upon successful registration, including the secure URL with the token.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that upon successful registration, the system sends a verification email containing the secure URL with the token to the user's email address.
Verify that if the email delivery service fails during initial sending, the system logs the error and returns a generic error message to the user without exposing delivery details.

sub_functions.description: Logs verification-related events for security and compliance, including token generation, verification attempts, and failures.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that a successful email verification event is logged with relevant details (e.g., user ID, timestamp) without exposing the raw token.
Verify that failed verification attempts (expired token, invalid token, system error) are logged with relevant details without exposing sensitive information.

main_functions.description: Handles login attempts for users who have not yet verified their email, preventing access and providing appropriate feedback.

sub_functions.description: When a user attempts to login without having verified their email, the system denies access and displays a message indicating that verification is required.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that a user with an unverified email cannot log in and receives a message indicating that email verification is required.
Verify that a user with a verified email can log in successfully.

main_functions.description: Automatically purges unverified user records from the database after 7 days to maintain database hygiene.

sub_functions.description: Runs a scheduled job to delete user records that have not been verified within 7 days of registration or last update.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that unverified records are deleted exactly 7 days after the initial registration timestamp.
Verify that unverified records are deleted exactly 7 days after the last update (re-registration) timestamp.
Verify that verified user records are not affected by the purge process and remain in the database.

main_functions.description: Allows users to reset their password via a secure email link, leveraging the existing verification infrastructure for security.

sub_functions.description: Handles the request for a password reset email, generating a secure token and sending it to the user's email.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that a password reset request for a verified email sends a secure link with a token to the user's email.
Verify that a password reset request for an unverified email returns a generic success message without revealing the verification status.
Verify that all password reset traffic is forced over HTTPS (TLS 1.3).
